en: Speaking of which, this isn't Havana in the 1950s, why do we have to wear tuxes?
What are you, Mo Green? Mo Green lived in Vegas. I know where Mo Green lived.
Complimentary champagne? You think this happens if you walk in here in a t-shirt and jeans?
You have a guest, Keith Hoyt. Can you let him know I'm here?
Mr. Hoyt left a message for you to meet him in the poker room, Mr. Spector.
The floor manager assured me that he'd be kept away from the tables.
I'm sorry, I have no record of that here, sir.
Double down? Okay, so let me get this straight. He has a gambling problem?
And drinking. Why is he in Atlantic City?
Because he's the keynote speaker at a clean energy conference, and he promised me he'd stay out of trouble.
And you took the word of an addict?
He's been clean five years. No drinking, no gambling.
So that's why you're here. You're concerned about him. Yes. But you also want to gamble.
But he has a problem. I do not have a problem.
Huh? Holy sh...
Look at this guy. Those are $25,000 chips. That's three stacks of four, 25 by seven.
That's Keith.
Well, at least we don't have to worry about him losing. All in.
Or... Keith. Harvey.
This is a bad idea. You haven't seen my hand.
I call.
Straight.
Full house. He's got one out.
We're fine, unless the river's a...
Ten of clubs.
That is some tough luck right there.
Tommy, please, just give me 48 hours. Sorry, Keith.
Deal's a deal. That was insane. You just lost $3 million.
Who the hell is that guy? He's worse than that napkin.
Harvey, I just lost my company.
